title: DHS Inspector General: Secretary Noem 'Systematically Obstructed' OIG Work
excerpt: DHS Inspector General Joseph Cuffari sent a letter to Congress asserting that Secretary Noem's DHS had systematically obstructed OIG investigations — including by withholding records across 10 active probes and revoking access to critical law enforcement databases.
content:
On March 2, 2026, DHS Inspector General Joseph Cuffari sent a formal letter to Congress detailing what he described as systematic obstruction of his office's work by DHS leadership under Secretary Kristi Noem.

  • The letter described 10 investigations — including immigration enforcement and use-of-force matters — in which DHS withheld records and placed restrictive conditions on OIG access to information.
  • DHS revoked OIG's long-standing access to the ICE Enforcement Integrated Database and a database tracking employee access to classified information — both essential for oversight of enforcement operations.
  • Cuffari reported that DHS General Counsel James Percival had 'repeatedly and unprompted' reminded OIG of the Secretary's statutory authority to prohibit investigations for national security or public interest — a power no DHS Secretary had ever previously invoked.
  • Senator Tammy Duckworth had earlier accused Noem of 'intimidating' Cuffari from probing Border Patrol use-of-force incidents following the Minneapolis shootings.
  • Percival attached a letter calling any OIG notification to Congress about access concerns 'bad faith and bordering on material misrepresentation.'

The Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee notified Percival it was opening its own investigation into Cuffari's allegations. The obstruction affected ongoing reviews of ICE excessive force and training — reviews announced just weeks earlier on February 5.
